hero.title: Chungking Express
hero.reason: A vibrant romance-drama that turns a random date into destiny (and makes you want to walk into the night like you're in a Wong Kar-wai montage).
hero.hook: Wong Kar-wai's masterpiece turns 32 today—a neon-soaked meditation on urban loneliness and chance encounters that defined '90s cinema.
hero.blurb: Two lovelorn cops drift through Hong Kong's glittering streets, each nursing a heartbreak in their own beautifully fractured way. This is visual poetry masquerading as a crime film—hypnotic, melancholic, and utterly unforgettable.

released.title: The Ballad of Little Jo
released.reason: A quietly radical western that’s stuck around because it’s tender, human, and genuinely different from the genre’s usual myths.
released.blurb: Maggie Greenwald's quiet, revisionist western follows a woman who disguises herself as a man to survive in 1880s Montana, working cattle ranches and claiming her own freedom. It's a tender, subversive, beautifully shot meditation on gender and belonging in the untamed frontier.

birthday.title: Thelma & Louise
birthday.reason: A go-for-broke classic tied to a real birthday today, and still one of the sharpest, most iconic rides in American cinema.
birthday.blurb: Though Zellweger isn't in this 1991 classic, she shares a birthday with director Ridley Scott, whose feminist road-trip thriller redefined the buddy movie. A searing, beautiful, devastating film about women refusing to apologize.

awful.title: Gigli
awful.reason: A famously disastrous studio comedy-romance that’s unintentionally mesmerizing when you can’t believe it’s real.
awful.blurb: Ben Affleck and Jennifer Lopez star in this incomprehensible mob-comedy-romance that somehow mistakes incoherence for edge. A film so aggressively unfunny it feels like a prank played on audiences worldwide.
